question: In one or two sentences, define machine learning and state its primary goal.
expectedPoints: A field of computer science that gives computers the ability to learn from data without being explicitly programmed.
The primary goal is to build systems that can improve their performance on a specific task through experience.

question: Explain how machine learning is both a subset of and distinct from the broader field of Artificial Intelligence.
expectedPoints: Machine Learning is a subset of AI, as it is one of the primary techniques used to achieve intelligent behavior.
It is distinct because not all AI uses learning; some systems operate based on hard-coded rules and logic (e.g., expert systems).
ML specifically focuses on developing systems that learn and adapt from data, whereas AI encompasses any technique that mimics intelligence.

question: Compare and contrast machine learning with traditional statistical modeling. Discuss their philosophical origins, primary objectives, and the treatment of model complexity.
expectedPoints: Philosophical Origins: Statistics is mathematics-driven, focused on inference and understanding data-generating processes. ML is computer science-driven, focused on prediction and algorithmic efficiency.
Primary Objective: Statistics aims to model the relationship between variables and test hypotheses (inference). ML aims to make accurate predictions on new, unseen data (prediction).

Treatment of Complexity: Statistics often prefers simpler, more interpretable models to avoid overfitting. ML readily employs highly complex models (e.g., deep learning) and uses techniques like regularization and validation to manage overfitting in pursuit of higher predictive accuracy.
